# Type Safety Checklist: SPEC-008 Perspectives

**Purpose**: Validate that Zod schema contracts, input/output types, discriminated union patterns, and shared identifier schemas are completely, clearly, and consistently specified for all 5 Perspective tools.
**Created**: 2026-03-18
**Feature**: specs/008-perspectives (spec.md, contracts/, data-model.md, plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness -- Zod Schema Coverage

- [ ] CHK001 - Are Zod input schemas defined and documented for all 5 tools (list_perspectives, get_perspective, switch_perspective, export_perspective, set_perspective_icon)? [Completeness, Spec FR-003/FR-009/FR-017/FR-025/FR-033]
- [ ] CHK002 - Are Zod output response schemas (success + error discriminated unions) defined for all 5 tools? [Completeness, Spec SC-005]
- [ ] CHK003 - Are all exported TypeScript types derived from `z.infer<>` (no manually authored interface types alongside schemas)? [Completeness, contracts/index.ts]
- [ ] CHK004 - Does the `index.ts` barrel export include all schemas AND their inferred types for each of the 5 tools plus shared modules? [Completeness, contracts/index.ts]
- [ ] CHK005 - Are the shared schemas (`PerspectiveIdentifierSchema`, `PerspectiveListItemSchema`, `CustomPerspectiveDetailSchema`, `BuiltInPerspectiveDetailSchema`, `PerspectiveTypeSchema`) each documented with their purpose and usage context? [Completeness, contracts/shared/]

## Requirement Completeness -- Input Schema Design

- [ ] CHK006 - Is the `PerspectiveIdentifierSchema` `.refine()` validation (at least one of name/identifier required) documented with the exact error message in requirements? [Completeness, perspective-identifier.ts]
- [ ] CHK007 - Does the spec define that `ListPerspectivesInputSchema` uses `.default('all')` on the `type` enum, so callers can omit the parameter entirely? [Completeness, Spec FR-003]
- [ ] CHK008 - Is the `ExportPerspectiveInputSchema` use of `.and()` intersection (PerspectiveIdentifier + saveTo) documented as the composition pattern? [Completeness, export-perspective.ts]
- [ ] CHK009 - Is the `SetPerspectiveIconInputSchema` use of `.and()` intersection (PerspectiveIdentifier + color) documented as the composition pattern? [Completeness, set-perspective-icon.ts]
- [ ] CHK010 - Are `.min(1)` constraints on string fields (name, identifier, saveTo, color) consistently specified to prevent empty string submissions? [Completeness, contracts/shared/perspective-identifier.ts]

## Requirement Clarity -- Response Schema Design

- [ ] CHK011 - Is the `list_perspectives` response requirement clear that `perspectives` is a flat array of `PerspectiveListItem` objects (not grouped into separate built-in/custom arrays)? [Clarity, Spec FR-006 vs list-perspectives.ts]
- [ ] CHK012 - Is it explicitly specified that `PerspectiveListItemSchema.identifier` is `string | null` (nullable for built-in, always present for custom)? [Clarity, perspective-summary.ts]
- [ ] CHK013 - Is it explicitly specified that `PerspectiveListItemSchema.filterAggregation` is `string | null` (nullable for built-in or OmniFocus < v4.2)? [Clarity, perspective-summary.ts]
- [ ] CHK014 - Are the two distinct success response variants for `export_perspective` (file success with `filePath` vs metadata success with `fileName`/`fileType`) clearly distinguished in requirements? [Clarity, Spec FR-029/FR-030]
- [ ] CHK015 - Is it specified how the discriminated union for `export_perspective` distinguishes between the two success variants at runtime (both have `success: true`)? [Clarity, export-perspective.ts]
- [ ] CHK016 - Is the `get_perspective` response union between `CustomPerspectiveDetail` and `BuiltInPerspectiveDetail` documented with how consumers discriminate the two (via the `type` literal field)? [Clarity, get-perspective.ts]

## Requirement Clarity -- Filter Rules Type

- [ ] CHK017 - Is the use of `z.unknown()` for `filterRules` explicitly justified in requirements as an opaque passthrough (not a type-safety compromise)? [Clarity, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK018 - Are requirements clear that `z.unknown()` accepts ANY JSON value from OmniFocus without runtime validation, and that this is intentional per KISS/YAGNI? [Clarity, plan.md AD]
- [ ] CHK019 - Is the `filterAggregation` field type specified as `string` (not an enum like `z.enum(['any', 'all', 'none'])`) and is this choice documented? [Clarity, perspective-summary.ts vs Spec FR-005]

## Requirement Consistency -- Disambiguation Pattern

- [ ] CHK020 - Is the disambiguation error shape (`candidates` array of `{name, identifier}` objects) consistent across all 4 tools that support disambiguation (get_perspective, switch_perspective, export_perspective, set_perspective_icon)? [Consistency, contracts/]
- [x] CHK021 - Does the spec acknowledge that the `candidates` shape (`{name, identifier}`) differs from the established codebase pattern of `matchingIds` (`string[]`) used in status-tools, folder-tools, and tag-tools? [Consistency, Gap] -- RESOLVED: Added Disambiguation Pattern section to data-model.md documenting the deviation and justification (built-in perspectives lack simple IDs; name+identifier pairs needed).
- [x] CHK022 - Is the deviation from `matchingIds` to `candidates` explicitly justified (e.g., perspectives lack a simple ID for built-in, need name+id pairs)? [Consistency, Gap] -- RESOLVED: Justification documented in data-model.md Disambiguation Pattern section with 3 specific reasons.
- [ ] CHK023 - Are the error `code` enums consistent and non-overlapping across tools: `NOT_FOUND` (4 tools), `NO_WINDOW` (switch), `BUILTIN_NOT_EXPORTABLE` (export), `BUILTIN_NOT_MODIFIABLE` (icon), `VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED` (icon), `INVALID_DIRECTORY` (export), `DISAMBIGUATION_REQUIRED` (4 tools)? [Consistency, contracts/]

## Requirement Consistency -- Shared Schema Reuse

- [ ] CHK024 - Is it specified that `GetPerspectiveInputSchema`, `SwitchPerspectiveInputSchema` reuse `PerspectiveIdentifierSchema` directly (not a copy), while `ExportPerspectiveInputSchema` and `SetPerspectiveIconInputSchema` extend it via `.and()`? [Consistency, contracts/]
- [ ] CHK025 - Does the `PerspectiveTypeSchema` (`z.enum(['builtin', 'custom'])`) serve as the single source of truth for type discrimination, used consistently across `PerspectiveListItemSchema` and `CustomPerspectiveDetailSchema`? [Consistency, perspective-identifier.ts vs perspective-summary.ts]
- [ ] CHK026 - Are the `BUILT_IN_PERSPECTIVE_NAMES` const array and `BuiltInPerspectiveName` type defined once and referenced consistently wherever built-in names are needed? [Consistency, perspective-identifier.ts]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality -- Schema Validation

- [ ] CHK027 - Are measurable acceptance criteria defined for Zod schema validation: what happens when invalid input is provided (e.g., empty name, missing both name and identifier, invalid hex color)? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec FR-036]
- [ ] CHK028 - Is the CSS hex color regex pattern (`/^#([0-9a-fA-F]{3}|[0-9a-fA-F]{4}|[0-9a-fA-F]{6}|[0-9a-fA-F]{8})$/`) documented with the exact formats accepted and rejected (e.g., "red", "FF0000" without #, "#GG0000")? [Acceptance Criteria, set-perspective-icon.ts]
- [ ] CHK029 - Are acceptance criteria defined for `totalCount` field validation: must it equal `perspectives.length`, and is this enforced in the schema or left as an implementation concern? [Acceptance Criteria, list-perspectives.ts]

## Scenario Coverage -- Discriminated Union Correctness

- [ ] CHK030 - Is it documented that `ExportPerspectiveResponseSchema` uses `z.discriminatedUnion('success', [...])` with THREE variants (two success, one error), and that Zod 4.x correctly handles multiple variants sharing the same discriminator value? [Coverage, export-perspective.ts]
- [ ] CHK031 - Are requirements specified for how `get_perspective` response parsing distinguishes `CustomPerspectiveDetail` from `BuiltInPerspectiveDetail` when both share `success: true` (nested `z.union()` on `perspective` field, discriminated by `type` literal)? [Coverage, get-perspective.ts]
- [ ] CHK032 - Is the `error.code` field specified as `.optional()` across all error schemas, and is it documented when `code` may be absent vs always present? [Coverage, contracts/]

## Edge Case Coverage -- Identifier Types

- [ ] CHK033 - Are requirements defined for what happens when an `identifier` is provided that matches a custom perspective, but a `name` is also provided that matches a different built-in perspective? [Edge Case, perspective-identifier.ts]
- [x] CHK034 - Is it specified whether `identifier` accepts any string format or only OmniFocus-generated identifier strings (e.g., UUID-like)? [Edge Case, Gap] -- RESOLVED: Documented in data-model.md PerspectiveIdentifier section: identifiers are opaque strings generated by OmniFocus (typically UUID-like). MCP server treats them as opaque -- no format validation beyond `.min(1)`.
- [ ] CHK035 - Are requirements defined for case sensitivity of the `identifier` field (exact match) vs the `name` field (case-insensitive for built-in, exact for custom)? [Edge Case, Spec FR-009/FR-010]
- [x] CHK036 - Is name lookup behavior specified for custom perspectives: is `Perspective.Custom.byName()` case-sensitive or case-insensitive? [Edge Case, Gap] -- RESOLVED: Documented in data-model.md and spec Assumptions: assumed case-sensitive for custom perspectives (consistent with other OmniJS byName() methods), to be verified in Script Editor during implementation.

## Edge Case Coverage -- Export Response Ambiguity

- [ ] CHK037 - Is the `fileType` field in `ExportPerspectiveMetadataSuccessSchema` specified with its expected value (e.g., "com.omnigroup.omnifocus.perspective" or similar UTI)? [Edge Case, export-perspective.ts]
- [ ] CHK038 - Is the `fileName` field specified with its expected format (e.g., perspective name + ".ofocus-perspective" extension)? [Edge Case, export-perspective.ts]

## Non-Functional Requirements -- Schema Consistency with Codebase

- [ ] CHK039 - Are requirements specified for where contract schemas live during development (specs/008-perspectives/contracts/) vs runtime (src/contracts/perspective-tools/), and is the copy/sync process documented? [Non-Functional, plan.md Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK040 - Is the Zod version requirement (4.x) documented given that `z.discriminatedUnion()` behavior may differ between Zod 3.x and 4.x? [Non-Functional, plan.md Technical Context]
- [x] CHK041 - Are requirements specified for the `z.describe()` annotations on schema fields, ensuring AI assistants receive meaningful MCP tool parameter descriptions? [Non-Functional, Gap] -- RESOLVED: Documented in data-model.md Notes: z.describe() annotations follow existing codebase conventions and are an implementation detail, not a spec-level concern. Contracts include .describe() calls consistent with existing tool schemas.

## Dependencies and Assumptions

- [ ] CHK042 - Is the assumption documented that `z.discriminatedUnion('success', [...])` with two same-discriminator-value variants (both `success: true` in export_perspective) is valid in Zod 4.x? [Assumption, export-perspective.ts]
- [ ] CHK043 - Is the assumption documented that `.and()` (intersection) is the correct Zod pattern for extending `PerspectiveIdentifierSchema` with additional fields, rather than `.merge()` or `.extend()`? [Assumption, export-perspective.ts/set-perspective-icon.ts]
- [ ] CHK044 - Is the dependency on the `.refine()` validation pattern (from TaskIdentifierSchema in notification-tools) explicitly referenced as the precedent? [Dependency, perspective-identifier.ts]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items CHK021, CHK022, CHK034, CHK036, CHK041 -- all gaps resolved during remediation pass (2026-03-18)
- Items CHK015, CHK030, CHK042, CHK043 are non-gap items that remain to be checked separately
- The `candidates` vs `matchingIds` deviation (CHK021-022) is now documented with explicit justification in data-model.md
- The `export_perspective` triple-variant discriminated union (CHK030, CHK042) is novel in this codebase and warrants explicit Zod 4.x compatibility documentation
